risk_rationale: The draft bill introduces new requirements for obtaining 'verifiable parental consent' and conditions for age verification data collection and use, which are not explicitly covered in TikTok's current compliance baseline. This represents a material compliance gap that requires immediate attention to ensure alignment with potential new legal standards.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states it uses machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users and remove/limit access.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Defines 'verifiable parental consent' and specifies conditions for age verification data collection and use.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not explicitly address the requirement for 'verifiable parental consent' or the specific conditions for collecting and using age verification data as outlined in the draft bill.

remediations.title: Implement Verifiable Parental Consent Mechanism
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a mechanism to obtain and verify parental consent in compliance with the new regulatory requirements. This should include a system for collecting and managing age verification data in accordance with the specified conditions.
acceptance_criteria: A verifiable parental consent mechanism is implemented and operational.
Age verification data collection and use comply with the new regulatory conditions.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new requirements could lead to significant legal challenges and potential fines, impacting TikTok's ability to operate in the US market.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed legal review of the draft bill to understand all implications.
Initiate a project to develop the necessary systems for compliance with the new requirements.
Engage with US regulatory bodies to clarify any ambiguities in the bill and ensure alignment with compliance efforts.
